content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Given how intertwined this is (my task requires refactoring _template_from_str/_template_from_file into a single centralized create_jinja_env() helper, which is exactly where your env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY) line would also need to live), I think a clean sub-file split is too fragile and will cause merge conflicts. Proposal: I take full ownership of outlines/prompts.py this round and implement both features together: my filters param (applied last, taking precedence) AND your register_filter()/unregister_filter() module-level registry with _FILTER_REGISTRY dict, applied first via create_jinja_env(). Please send me your exact intended function signatures/docstrings/behavior for register_filter and unregister_filter (e.g. do they overwrite, raise on duplicate, return anything?) and I will implement them verbatim, then send you the full diff for your review/ACCEPT before I finalize. If you would strongly prefer to own the file instead and implement my filters param yourself, let me know and send me the file back when done - either way works for me.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented: prompt decorator now supports @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}); Prompt.from_str/from_file accept filters=; added create_jinja_env(loader, filters) helper used by _template_from_str/_template_from_file, with custom filters applied via env.filters.update(filters) after the built-in ones (so custom filters override built-ins on name clash). All 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass. Done on my end.
